steps.title: Extract the three data points from the table
steps.content:
The table gives three $(x, y)$ pairs:
  • $(x, y) = (-2s, 28)$
  • $(x, y) = (-s, 23)$
  • $(x, y) = (s, 13)$
Since there is a linear relationship, the equation must satisfy all three points.

steps.title: Backsolving — test choice B
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B ($5x + sy = 18s$):
  • Point $(-2s, 28)$: $5(-2s) + s(28) = -10s + 28s = 18s$ ✓
  • Point $(-s, 23)$: $5(-s) + s(23) = -5s + 23s = 18s$ ✓
  • Point $(s, 13)$: $5(s) + s(13) = 5s + 13s = 18s$ ✓
All three points satisfy the equation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
